svg.text: Auto-detect when combo components are added to an order → apply bundle price automatically. Saves staff time, maximizes upsell.
per order vs a la carte
pos_combos.id = COMBO-001 · auto_detect: true · 47 orders today
NAME (name_i18n jsonb — EN / ES / FR / ZH)
Classic Burger $18 · Veggie Burger $15 · Chicken Burger $16 · +10 more
Automatic — system offers combo when all required components in order
Staff-triggered — staff selects "Add as Combo" manually
Both — auto-detect AND staff can manually trigger
OFF — all required components must be present
ON — suggest combo if only some components ordered
"These items qualify for Burger Combo! Save $3.51"
pos_combos: id, name_i18n jsonb, price_cents int, active bool, auto_detect bool, channel_flags jsonb, created_at timestamptz
pos_combo_components: id, combo_id → pos_combos, component_type (category | specific), item_ids int[], required bool, sort_order int
Detection: on each pos_order_items INSERT → check_combos() Edge Function → find matching combos where all required components present AND not already combo-priced
→ fire combo_offer event to terminal WebSocket → register renders pop-up → staff/customer accepts → price_override applied
Apply: pos_order_items grouped by combo_id, price_override = combo_price_cents / components (for reporting granularity). KDS receives individual item tickets.
Receipt: combo name as header line + each component as indented sub-line. Reports: "Burger Combo × 1 $14.99" single line — drill-down shows component split.
Upsell edge fn: if order has ≥1 component of combo but not all → push upsell_suggestion event → register shows "Add X for Burger Combo $14.99, save $11.01" CTA.
